frameworks.name: dual-store theory
frameworks.one_line: separate short-term and long-term stores drive recall behavior

frameworks.name: retrieved context theory
frameworks.one_line: memory search uses an evolving contextual cue bound to studied items

frameworks.name: computational models of memory search
frameworks.one_line: formal models explaining how the brain searches memory for context-bound events

core_question: How is memory search for events learned in a particular context computationally accomplished?
key_assumptions: memory search can be formalized via computational models
context plays a central role in episodic retrieval
benchmark recall phenomena constrain candidate theories
key_findings: dual-store theory accounts for a set of benchmark memory-search findings
retrieved context theory accounts for memory-search benchmarks through evolving context
the two influential approaches differ in their core theoretical commitments
interpretations: comparing dual-store and retrieved context models clarifies key theoretical ideas about memory search
open questions remain about the mechanisms underlying memory search
limitations_noted: important open questions remain to be addressed by future research
